items.title: Private credit worries resurface in $3 trillion market as AI pressures software firms
items.description: Artificial intelligence is adding a new layer of uncertainty to the private credit industry, raising concerns that some lenders may face rising defaults.

items.title: What The Latest Price Target Tweaks Mean For Smith Douglas Homes (SDHC) Story
items.description: Smith Douglas Homes just saw its fair value estimate reaffirmed at US$16.50, even as the headline price target moved to US$17 in recent research. That small adjustment reflects analysts fine tuning their view as they balance solid return on equity and medium term growth prospects with a more Neutral stance on upside. Stay tuned as we walk through what is driving these price target tweaks and how you can keep on top of this evolving story going forward. Analyst Price Targets don't always...

items.title: IVV’s Uninterrupted Dividend Streak Since 2000 Masks The True Income Profile
items.description: The iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:IVV) generates income the straightforward way: it holds the stocks of the 500 largest U.S. companies and passes through the dividends they pay. With $763.6 billion in net assets and an expense ratio of just 0.03%, IVV offers efficient exposure to the entire S&P 500. The fund’s dividend safety ... IVV’s Uninterrupted Dividend Streak Since 2000 Masks The True Income Profile

items.title: Why The Narrative Around Middleby (MIDD) Is Shifting On Mixed Fair Value Signals
items.description: Why Middleby’s Updated Fair Value Is Only Slightly Higher Middleby’s fair value estimate has been nudged up from US$164.25 to US$166.88, a small change that reflects how closely analysts are weighing fresh research rather than a major shift in the story. With one large firm trimming its price target to US$168 and another, JPMorgan, lifting its own, the modest move in fair value captures that mix of optimism and caution you are seeing across recent reports. Stay tuned to see how you can track...

items.title: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Is Up 5.4% After Winning Apple Card Issuance From Goldman Sachs
items.description: In early 2026, Apple selected JPMorgan Chase to replace Goldman Sachs as the issuer of the Apple Card, shifting over 12 million existing cardholders to Chase’s platform. This move gives JPMorgan a large influx of digitally engaged customers and fresh opportunities to deepen relationships across its broader consumer banking and payments ecosystem. Next, we’ll examine how inheriting Apple Card’s sizeable user base could reshape JPMorgan Chase’s investment narrative around scale and consumer...
